notes: Step 1 of ILM recipe; SANs cited as affordable even for small enterprises by 2004.
Supplemental to SAN in the centralized storage model; became ubiquitous as predicted.
Online pool disk interface for OLTP and high-activity DSS workloads.
Aberdeen-coined term for capacity-oriented ATA/SATA disks at ~25% of FC/SCSI cost; emerged as bulk data tier. Wikibon 2011: 85% of enterprise data on SATA/midline at 40% of spend.
Disk-to-disk backup and restore pool complementing tape; terminology persists in enterprise vocabulary today.
Offline tier for disaster recovery; cited as enormous litigation liability if data retention policy not followed.
Cross-application automated ILM software predicted to mature circa 2008-2010; prediction proved correct — EMC, NetApp, HP all shipped mature solutions 2009-2011.
Cited as becoming embedded; predicted to drive economies of scale; became standard feature in enterprise storage.
Online dynamic data pool for highest-performance transaction processing workloads.
Cited as compressing backup times by up to 50%; part of nearline pool evolution.
